description: Bug Fixes - Admin Navigation, Class/Session Actions, Terms Link Hostname

userStories.title: Fix terms link hostname in WhatsApp messages
userStories.description: As a customer, I need the terms acceptance link in WhatsApp to use the correct hostname so I can access the terms page without encountering invalid-host errors.
acceptanceCriteria: WhatsApp message generation reads hostname from APP_BASE_URL environment variable
If APP_BASE_URL is not set, throw error at message-send time (fail-fast, do not use hardcoded fallback)
Terms link format: https://{APP_BASE_URL}/terms?token={uuid}
Link must be absolute URL (not relative path)
Link must include token query parameter with valid purchase token
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add persistent admin navigation component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need visible navigation after login so I can switch between admin pages without using the browser back button or manually typing URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create AdminNav component with links to: Dashboard, Classes, Sessions, Participants, Terms
Add AdminNav to admin layout so it appears on all admin pages
Navigation uses semantic HTML (nav element, a tags with proper hrefs)
Active page has visual indicator (highlight current route)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add edit button for classes (super admin only)
userStories.description: As a super admin, I need an Edit button on each class row so I can update class name/description when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit button visible in each class row on /admin/classes page
Button only visible when logged in as super_admin role
Clicking Edit opens modal with current class data pre-filled (name, description)
Saving the form updates the class record in database
Updated values display immediately after save
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add cancel button for classes with future session guard
userStories.description: As a super admin, I need a Cancel button for each class so I can mark a class as cancelled when it's no longer offered, with validation to prevent cancelling classes with active future sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel button visible in each class row on /admin/classes page
Button only visible when logged in as super_admin role
Clicking Cancel opens confirmation dialog
System checks for active future sessions (status !== 'cancelled' AND date >= today)
If active future sessions exist, show error and prevent cancellation
If no active future sessions, update class.status to cancelled
Cancelled classes show visual indicator (grayed out or badge)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add edit button for sessions (super admin only)
userStories.description: As a super admin, I need an Edit button on each session row so I can update session details when schedules change.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit button visible in each session row on session list page
Button only visible when logged in as super_admin role
Clicking Edit opens modal with current session data pre-filled (location, date, time, quota_defined)
Saving the form updates the session record in database
Updated values display immediately after save
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add cancel button for sessions (super admin only)
userStories.description: As a super admin, I need a Cancel button for each session so I can mark a session as cancelled when it won't occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel button visible in each session row on session list page
Button only visible when logged in as super_admin role
Clicking Cancel opens confirmation dialog
After confirmation, update session.status to cancelled
Cancelled sessions excluded from terms acceptance session selector dropdown
Cancelled sessions show visual indicator in admin session list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Verify role-based action button visibility
userStories.description: As a regular admin, I should NOT see edit/cancel buttons so I understand my view-only access level.
acceptanceCriteria: When logged in as regular_admin, no Edit or Cancel buttons visible on classes page
When logged in as regular_admin, no Edit or Cancel buttons visible on sessions page
When logged in as super_admin, all Edit and Cancel buttons are visible
Role checks enforced both client-side (visibility) and server-side (mutations)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
